title: Extracting formula value using Make
excerpt: Using make, how do I get the value from a formula column in monday?

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Using make, how do I get the value from a formula column in monday?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: This is not possible, formulas are currently calculated within your browser window. The values of formulas are not stored in the monday backend.

posts.author: hellotimi (David Kalu (Formula Pro - Use formula column results in automations!))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Soroh,

We built an app that solves this exact pain point of extracting formula values and using it in automations, it’s called Formula PRO.

With Formula PRO, you can project and write the results from your native formula columns to other columns (including those not supported by the native formula column), as well as to mirror columns and subitems.

You can use recipes like these to achieve the results you want:

Screenshot 2024-12-20 at 14.39.152438×648 37.6 KB

Screenshot 2024-12-20 at 15.07.282438×648 39.8 KB

And there are many more recipes available on the app.

We just launched it last week and we plan to keep adding more features on a bi-weekly basis.

You can check it out here: Formula PRO : Advanced Automations for Native Formula Columns, Mirror Columns & Subitems!

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Using Monday’s newest API version – 2025-01, this can be done via the API.

posts.author: Jasmark (Jay)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve tried this and it doesn’t seem to work? I’ve tried extracting to a number and text column.
